what_it_does: Aggregates six read-only research-agent findings into one landscape document for a proposed Concurrent Cognition subsystem, describing the architecture, reusable substrate, net-new build items, open design forks, and a recommended proving spike.
observations: Frames a turn reply as a staged stream of parts, each part spoken by the resident 4B model when its input addresses resolve.
Proposes a swarm of ~32 concurrent role-runs against the single resident 4B, relying on vLLM server-side batching rather than client-side batching.
Lists existing capabilities with file/line evidence, including the scheduler, role registry, injection/resolution path, modes, structured output, SSE stream, and canvas rendering.
Enumerates ten net-new build items, starting with parallel dispatch and ending with cognition rendering.
Identifies six distinct pieces and names the VRAM-bounded slot scheduler on the one resident model as the key constraint.
Surfaces open design forks that require Tim's explicit decision, such as part grain, first role cast, reserved slot count, address scheme, and render approach.
Recommends a proving spike: generalise the judge into a 2-role registry that feeds one injected second part before fanning out to the full swarm.
standouts: The resident 4B is intended to serve as both the main voice-stream generator and the host for ~32 concurrent internal role-runs.
The judge is explicitly cast as role #0, completing work already declared in the ROLE_REGISTRY comment.
Mode is treated as a single dial that gates staging, selects thought-shape, selects the role set, and sets the slot budget.
The swarm is described as internal cognition off the MCP face, not an operator-facing verb.
conventions: References existing code with file:line ranges and function/class names.
Uses project-specific terms such as address→resolve→inject, run://, swarm://, RHM, MCP, SSE, and reflects-never-owns.
Structures content with tables, ASCII diagrams, and numbered priority lists.
Maintains a neutral, non-ranking interpretive stance as requested.
concerns: Relies on unverified benchmark claims about feasibility at concurrency 32 and reliable 4B strict-schema output.
Contains many open forks that could block implementation until Tim decides.
The file is a planning/aggregation document; its claims about existing code behavior are not verified within the file.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the consolidated research map for turning the existing serial pipeline into an intra-turn concurrent-cognition swarm, with a clear proving-spike next step.
questions: What are the exact contents of the six agent findings (01–06) that this document aggregates?
Where is the benchmark evidence supporting the concurrency-32 and json-schema reliability claims?
What are the precise current behaviors of ROLE_REGISTRY, MODES, _resolve_context_at, and the scheduler as implemented?
How would the proposed swarm:// namespace coexist or conflict with the existing run:// addressing scheme?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to aggregate research findings into one picture; actual content matches by covering architecture, reuse, net-new components, open forks, and the path forward.
apparent_themes: Intra-turn parallel cognition as a natural extension of the existing reactive scheduler relationship.
Additive completion of declared registry work rather than a separate parallel system.
Attention economics: mode as a single dial that controls staging, shape, role set, and slot budget.
Reflects-never-owns rendering and live SSE-driven UI updates.
Prove the end-to-end mechanism before scaling to the full swarm.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to justify and plan a build that reuses the existing graph, scheduler, role registry, and injection machinery to stage multi-part replies, using concurrent role-runs on a single resident model, while forcing all major design decisions to be made explicitly with Tim.
novelty: The proposal to use ~32 concurrent requests to one resident 4B model to produce a staged, multi-part reply, where role outputs are injected as resolved context for the next part, and where mode is treated as a unified attention-budget dial.
contribution: A structured landscape that links the proposed concurrent-cognition architecture to existing code locations, enumerates the net-new components needed, flags decision forks requiring human input, and proposes a minimal end-to-end proving spike to validate the mechanism before broader implementation.
summary_for_embedding: Aggregated research landscape for a proposed intra-turn concurrent-cognition swarm that stages multi-part replies via ~32 concurrent role-runs on one resident 4B, mapping existing reuse, net-new build items, open forks, and a proving spike.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate six agent research threads into a single build-prep map for Tim and the autonomous build loop.
